data.content: Found the issue. The `.sln` is missing `ProjectConfigurationPlatforms` — no projects are actually being built. Let me verify:

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-8238f048c9ac4b0aada0828fb02fa6b3 && dotnet build Core/Core.csproj -v minimal 2>&1 && echo "---" && find . -name "Core.dll" -path "*/bin/*"

data.reasoningText:
The solution file is missing the `ProjectConfigurationPlatforms` section! Without `GlobalSection(ProjectConfigurationPlatforms) = postSolution`, MSBuild doesn't know which projects to build for each solution configuration. That's why it says "Build succeeded" but produces no output - no projects are configured to build.

This is actually the bottleneck/issue. The solution file is missing the project configuration mapping, so no projects get built when building the solution. Let me verify by building individual projects.
